scenarios.brief: You are MV Blue Duck (MMSI: *********). Engine room fire is out of control. 6 persons on board, position 32°05'N 034°45'E. Ship is in immediate danger.
You are SY Sea Otter (MMSI: *********). Hit a submerged object, hull breached, taking water fast. 4 persons on board, position 31°50'N 034°30'E. Vessel will sink within minutes.
You are MV Northern Star (MMSI: *********). Collided with another vessel, severe damage to bow, taking water. 12 persons on board, position 33°10'N 035°02'E.

facts.nature: Collided with another vessel and taking water

scenarios.brief: You are MV River Hawk (MMSI: *********). Engine-room fire has spread to the bridge and is uncontrollable. 7 persons on board, position 32°25'N 034°48'E. Make the MAYDAY call — you must abandon ship to the liferaft immediately after.

facts.nature: Fire out of control, abandoning ship

scenarios.brief: You are MV Aurora (MMSI: *********). Catastrophic hull breach after grounding, vessel listing heavily and capsizing. 9 persons on board, position 31°40'N 034°25'E. Make the MAYDAY call — you must abandon ship to the liferaft immediately after.

You are SY Wandering Albatross (MMSI: *********). Mast came down in heavy weather and is lying over the side with rigging in the water. Vessel disabled and drifting. 4 persons on board, position 32°35'N 034°15'E.

You are SY Jasmin (MMSI 428 070 371), authorised for Area A1 and fitted to GMDSS requirements. In a heavy, rough sea the mast has come down and is over the side with rigging in the water; the yacht is uncontrollable and in danger of sinking. 11 persons on board, position 34°41'N 034°11'E. Make the distress call — be ready to abandon to the liferaft if she goes down.

facts.nature: Dismasted, disabled and drifting, in danger of sinking

scenarios.brief: You are the vessel Amalia (MMSI 234 555 044), authorised for Area A1 and fitted to GMDSS requirements. Leaving the Strait of Gibraltar westbound at 00:40 local time, she has struck a submerged rock; water is coming in and the vessel and her 6 crew are in danger. The skipper orders you to get help immediately — be ready to abandon to the liferaft if she goes down. Position 36°20'N 006°24'W.

facts.nature: Grounded on a submerged reef, flooding and in danger of sinking

scenarios.brief: You are the vessel Aviva (MMSI 428 555 044), authorised for Area A1 and fitted to GMDSS requirements. West of Ashdod a fire has broken out on board and the vessel and her 10 crew are in danger. The skipper has not ordered abandonment, but you must get help immediately and be ready to abandon to the liferaft if she has to be left. Position 31°48'N 034°33'E.

facts.nature: Fire on board, in danger of sinking

scenarios.brief: You are SY Tabor (MMSI 428 000 666), authorised for Area A1 and fitted to GMDSS requirements. A 35-year-old male passenger is missing and is believed to have fallen overboard; he was last seen today at 01:30 UTC. 9 persons on board, position 36°24'N 025°28'E, steering course 043° at 6 knots. The skipper orders you to call for immediate help.

facts.nature: Man overboard — male, age 35, last seen 01:30 UTC, course 043°, speed 6 knots

scenarios.brief: You are SY Red Fox (MMSI: *********). Engine has failed, drifting toward a traffic separation scheme. Not in immediate danger, but need a tow. 3 persons on board, position 32°15'N 034°40'E.

You are MV Grey Whale (MMSI: *********). Crew member with severe chest pain — suspected cardiac event. Address RCC Haifa. 8 persons on board, position 31°45'N 034°20'E. Patient: male, 52, temp 37.2°C, BP 160/100, conscious but very weak. Crew has given aspirin and rigged oxygen. Make the MEDICO call (DSC + Ch 16), then continue on the working channel.

facts.patientStatus: Severe chest pain, conscious but very weak, suspected cardiac event
facts.actionsTaken: Aspirin administered, oxygen rigged, patient resting

scenarios.brief: You are MV Cargo Ranger (MMSI: *********). A crew member fell from the upper deck and is unconscious. No nearby coast station designated — broadcast to All Stations. 14 persons on board, position 32°50'N 035°10'E. Patient: male, 28, temp 36.0°C, BP 90/60, unresponsive with suspected head injury. Crew immobilised the head/neck and is monitoring breathing. Make the MEDICO call (DSC + Ch 16), then continue on the working channel.

facts.patientStatus: Unconscious after fall from upper deck, suspected head injury
facts.actionsTaken: Head and neck immobilised, breathing monitored, no medication given

scenarios.brief: You are SY Blue Marlin (MMSI: *********). A crew member has uncontrolled bleeding from a deep galley laceration. Address RCC Haifa. 5 persons on board, position 32°10'N 034°35'E. Patient: female, 41, temp 36.8°C, BP 100/65, conscious and pale. Direct pressure applied, tourniquet rigged on upper arm. Make the MEDICO call (DSC + Ch 16), then continue on the working channel.

facts.patientStatus: Uncontrolled bleeding from deep galley laceration, conscious and pale
facts.actionsTaken: Direct pressure applied, tourniquet rigged on upper arm

scenarios.brief: You are MV Silver Arrow (MMSI: *********). Lost steering, vessel manoeuvring with difficulty in busy shipping lane. Position 32°30'N 034°55'E.

facts.assistance: Request other vessels to keep clear

scenarios.brief: You are MV Cape Runner. Sighted a partly submerged shipping container drifting in a busy shipping lane, hazard to navigation. Position 32°20'N 034°50'E.
You are MV Osprey. Lateral buoy reported unlit at the harbour approach. Position 31°55'N 034°35'E.
You are MV Storm Petrel. Observed sudden gale-force squalls in the area, hazard to small craft. Position 33°00'N 035°00'E.
You abandoned ship and are aboard the Albatross life raft. Your Radar SART has just activated and is being tracked by passing ships. 4 persons on board. Make the distress call.

facts.sartAddressee: Ship who received my Radar SART

scenarios.brief: You are aboard the Petrel life raft and sight a red-hulled cargo ship on the horizon, off the ship's starboard side. 5 persons on board. Make the distress call to the sighted ship.

facts.position: I am on your starboard side

scenarios.brief: RCC Haifa acknowledged your earlier MAYDAY. Respond to RCC and request fast vessels to evacuate your crew. You are sailboat Sea Otter.

facts.actionRequest: Send fast ships to the distress area to evacuate the crew

scenarios.brief: You are MV Vered (MMSI: *********). You overheard a MAYDAY distress call from Yacht Tami (MMSI 428 555 222) on Channel 16: position 33°42'N 032°52'E, fire on board in danger of sinking, 6 persons on board. The call has gone unacknowledged for over 5 minutes. Address the relay to All Stations.

facts.relayedNature: Fire on board in danger of sinking

scenarios.brief: You are MV Northern Falcon (MMSI: *********). You overheard a MAYDAY distress call from MV Coral Bay (MMSI 428 777 888) on Channel 16: position 32°15'N 034°20'E, collision with another vessel, taking water fast, 12 persons on board. RCC Haifa requested you relay the distress. Address the relay to RCC Haifa.

facts.relayedNature: Collision with another vessel, taking water fast

scenarios.brief: You are MV Carmel Trader (MMSI: *********), a few miles from the passenger ship Mediterranean Star. A major fire on her bridge has destroyed her main radio installation — she cannot transmit her own distress alert. Survivors in her liferafts reach you on a portable VHF: 240 persons on board, position 32°48'N 034°58'E, and no station has answered. They ask you to relay their MAYDAY. Address the relay to All Stations.

facts.relayedNature: Major fire on the bridge, main radio destroyed

scenarios.brief: You are SY Horizon (MMSI 428 150 052), at the scene of the passenger ship Pacific (MMSI 209 221 000), which is on fire. A fire on her bridge has destroyed her radio installation — she cannot transmit her own distress alert. Her MAYDAY heard on Channel 16 gave: position 13°30'N 050°15'W, on fire, 365 persons on board, requiring immediate assistance, EPIRB activated. No station has acknowledged. Relay her MAYDAY. Address the relay to All Stations.

facts.relayedNature: On fire, unable to operate radio equipment

scenarios.brief: You are MV Vered (MMSI: *********), in the vicinity of a position about 8 NM south of you. Five minutes ago you heard a MAYDAY on Channel 16 from Yacht Tami (MMSI 428 555 222) — fire on board, 6 persons aboard. No coast station has come back. You can still hear Tami calling.

You are SY Horizon (MMSI 428 150 052), at the scene. On Channel 16 you hear a MAYDAY from the passenger ship Pacific (MMSI 209 221 000): position 13°30'N 050°15'W, on fire, 365 persons on board, EPIRB activated and unable to use her radio station. No coast station has acknowledged. Respond to the distress call.

You are SY Sea Sprite (MMSI 428 070 555), authorised for Area A1 and fitted to GMDSS requirements. You have just sailed from Haifa bound for Limassol with 8 persons on board, ETA 0600 UTC tomorrow. Haifa Radio asks departing vessels to report their voyage so they can be tracked and a search launched if they fail to arrive. Position 32°49'N 035°00'E. Make the call to Haifa Radio.

facts.voyage: Bound Haifa to Limassol, ETA 0600 UTC tomorrow
